events.title: 2026 Fine Arts Center Gala
events.description: A long time ago, in a galaxy not so far away, the UMass Amherst Fine Arts Center was established - a force for creativity, culture, and community. Now, 50 years later, we invite you to join us for an evening honoring the past and igniting the next era of the arts. Enjoy stellar cuisine and libations, a live auction, and a dance party that is truly out of this world. Saturday, March 21, 2026 6:00 - 11:30pm UMass Amherst Student Union Ballroom Tickets: $150* per person *Includes $100 tax deductible gift to the Fine Arts Center Sponsorships: Starting at $1500 For more information about sponsorshi

events.title: Barry Lyndon
events.description: Ryan O’Neal stars in this gorgeous adaptation of Thackeray’s novel about the rise and fall of a sensitive and dashing Irish rogue. Kubrick evokes the light and texture of eighteenth-century painting with the help of pioneering cinematographic techniques and lavish costume and production design.

events.description: Prepare to be an active bystander when you see a situation that doesn’t feel right. Center for Women and Community's (CWC’s) Support Skill Building Series: Drop in for 1, 2 or all 3 Small Group Workshops to practice strategies to support yourself, your friends, and your community in times of stress or crisis.

events.description: The Office of Global Affairs will be hosting a monthly town hall for the international community to share information or updates to federal policy and hear community concerns.

events.title: Son of Monarchs
events.description: A Mexican biologist returns to his hometown amidst the butterfly forests of Michoacán, sparking a personal metamorphosis. Zoom introduction by Alexis Gambis, filmmaker, biologist, and founder of LABOCINE.

events.title: Days of Dialogue
events.description: Do you want to meet and connect with others on campus? The campuswide Intergroup Dialogue Initiative (IGDI) is pleased to invite all UMass Amherst students, faculty, and staff to join facilitated small-group circles during our Days of Dialogue—March 24-26. Register here These dialogue, listening, and storytelling circles invite members of our university community to share and consider similar and different perspectives on a particular topic in small groups. The topics are: Listening Circles: How Are We Each Navigating Political Polarization in our Families, Classrooms, Workplaces, & Communitie

events.title: Make a Button @ Innermission
events.description: Create calm in your day. Drop in Tuesday 3/24 to make a custom button. Visit Innermission M-F 11am - 4pm for self care activities like painting, journaling, and more.

events.title: Adoption Across the Life Course: The Needs and Experiences of Adult Adoptees in the USA and UK
events.description: Adoption across the life course The Rudd Adoption Research Program will be hosting a multi-date webinar series titled Adoption Across the Life Course: The Needs and Experiences of Adult Adoptees in the USA and UK. The webinars will feature five recent research studies from both the US and the UK. These will focus on different aspects of life for adopted people as adults (neurodiversity; becoming a parent; adoption consciousness; mental health; use of social media) as well as messages for the policy and practice. Each session will include adult adoptees speaking about their lived experiences. T

events.title: Isenberg Graduate Application Workshop
events.description: Join our Recruitment Specialist Team to learn more about the application process for Isenberg graduate programs. This webinar will cover tips for submitting a successful application, navigating the online application portal, and FAQs about admissions. We will discuss the application process for Isenberg’s MBA, MS in Business Analytics, MS in Accounting, Master of Finance, and dual degree programs. We will be giving away one application fee waiver to a lucky registrant of the session. Register today!

events.title: America's Conflicting Constitutional Values: The Quest for Common Ground
events.description: America’s political polarization is often fueled by clashes between two rival visions of the Constitution—one traditionalist, one progressive. Yet both interpretations are deeply rooted in the Constitution itself. In this thought-provoking lecture, Rogers M. Smith—a leading scholar of American constitutional law and political thought—explores how the Reconstruction Amendments—the 13th, 14th, and 15th—embody elements of both visions, and how they may offer a shared sense of national purpose that can guide us toward common ground today. Join us for an evening of reflection, history, and dialogue

events.title: Drip Like Coffee: The Craft of Black Lesbian Filmmaking
events.description: Join Anaiis Cisco, Smith College Assistant Professor of Film and Media Studies, for a one-hour talk tracing the history and aesthetics of Black lesbian filmmaking. Tickets to this Smith College Chaired Professor Lecture are free to the general public.

events.title: 2026 Distinguished Annual Lecture in History: Africa’s Struggle for a Place in the World
events.description: The UMass Amherst/Five College Graduate Program in History's 2026 Visiting Writer, Howard W. French, will give a lecture drawing from his acclaimed 2025 book The Second Emancipation: Nkrumah, Pan-Africanism, and Global Blackness at High Tide. French will explore the deep history of Pan-Africanism, as well as Africa’s effaced centrality in 20th century history, from the world wars to the U.S. civil rights movement and the global education of Ghanaian politician Kwame Nkrumah. This event is free and open to the public and a book signing will follow, with books available for purchase courtesy of

events.title: Issues & Clay: i feel it in my nerves: It gives me goosebumps
events.description: Join us for a conversation with UMass Professor Linnea Evans on how social exclusion processes affect the body’s physiological stress response system. Explore this topic with Professor Evans while you mold some air-dry clay. Get creative as we engage in these important topics. Event by Women of Color Leadership Network.

events.title: BPQC Meeting: Queer-Coded Hobbies
events.description: The Bi/Pan/Queer Collective (BPQC), a social group for bi, pan, queer, and sexually fluid students, will be discussing queer-coded hobbies at its next meeting. Open to all students.

events.title: Libraries' Page to Screen Club
events.description: Drop in to the Libraries' multimedia book club, where we read a book and then watch the screen adaptation! We meet for several weeks to talk about the book, then watch the screen adaptation together and compare. All sessions are 7-8:30 p.m. except for screenings, which are 7-9:30 p.m. The breakdown for what pages/sections to read will be determined at a later planning session. Little Women, Louisa May Alcott February 3 February 10 February 17 February 24 March 3 - Screening Stories of Your Life and Others, Ted Chiang March 10 March 17 - No meeting; spring vacation March 24 - Screening Jurassic

events.title: Infosession: MS in Computer Science — Online
events.description: This UMass Amherst online program allows you to earn your degree fully online while receiving the same rigorous education as our top-ranked in-person program. Attend a virtual information session to learn more. Visit here for more information and to register.

events.title: UMass History Visit Day
events.description: The Department of History and Transfer Admissions at UMass Amherst welcome students from MA community colleges to explore opportunities for studying history at UMass! Join us on campus to meet history students, faculty and staff; connect with transfer advisors; visit history classes; learn about the transfer process and majoring in history; tour the campus; explore our library & archives, including research opportunities for students; and enjoy a free lunch! Register by March 20 for priority registration at the link provided.

events.title: Cure
events.description: Kiyoshi Kurosawa’s spellbinding international breakthrough established him as one of the leaders of the emerging new wave of Japanese horror, while pushing the genre into uncharted realms of philosophical and existential exploration.

events.title: Caravaggio
events.description: Five years in production, this dramatic biography is the most extensive film ever made about one of the greatest artists of all time—Caravaggio.

events.title: Very Hungry Caterpillar Day
events.venue: Eric Carle Museum of Picture Book Art
events.description: Celebrate Very Hungry Caterpillar Day at The Carle!

events.title: Dog Day Afternoon
events.description: In August 1972, three amateur crooks in Brooklyn plan a nice, simple bank robbery. As everything goes wrong, the uncomplicated heist becomes a bizarre nightmare. Al Pacino and John Cazale star in director Sidney Lumet’s marvelously successful experiment in realism, featuring scenes of extended improvisation.

events.title: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
events.description: When their relationship sours, Joel and Clementine (Jim Carrey and Kate Winslet) undergo a medical procedure to have each other erased from their memories. Introduction by Rebecca Spencer, Professor of Psychological & Brain Sciences, UMass Amherst.

events.title: Dog Day Afternoon
events.description: In August 1972, three amateur crooks in Brooklyn plan a nice, simple bank robbery. As everything goes wrong, the uncomplicated heist becomes a bizarre nightmare. Al Pacino and John Cazale star in director Sidney Lumet’s marvelously successful experiment in realism, featuring scenes of extended improvisation.

events.title: When the Phone Rang
events.description: Through an intimate reconstruction of an important phone call, this film investigates dislocation and the nature of remembering.

events.title: A Night of Fairies
events.venue: Eric Carle Museum of Picture Book Art
events.description: As part of The Carle After Dark enjoy live music, make your own fairy house, and more!

events.title: Exploring Indigenous Stories in Picture Books
events.venue: Eric Carle Museum of Picture Book Art
events.description: Learn how to select and share picture books that celebrate Indigenous identities and cultures.

events.title: Romy and Michele's High School Reunion
events.description: Romy (Mira Sorvino) and Michele (Lisa Kudrow) are carefree party girls who reinvent themselves for their 10-year high school reunion.

events.title: Serpent's Path (1998) + Chime (2024)
events.description: After his 1997 breakthrough CURE, Kyoshi Kurosawa directed this dark gangland thriller following a yakuza subordinate who recruits a brilliant math teacher to help carry out a revenge scheme. New 4K restoration. Plays with CHIME, a 45-minute 2024 featurette from Kurosawa.

events.title: Serpent's Path (1998) + Chime (2024)
events.description: After his 1997 breakthrough CURE, Kyoshi Kurosawa directed this dark gangland thriller following a yakuza subordinate who recruits a brilliant math teacher to help carry out a revenge scheme. New 4K restoration. Plays with CHIME, a 45-minute 2024 featurette from Kurosawa.

events.title: Powwow People
events.description: POWWOW PEOPLE is a vérité-style documentary grounded in the rhythms, relationships, and lived experience of a contemporary Native gathering. Filmmaker Sky Hopinka joins us for a post-film discussion. Free to Amherst Cinema Members.

events.title: Tales From the Crypt: Demon Knight
events.description: In a New Mexico boarding house, ex-soldier Frank Brayker, guardian of an ancient key that can unlock tremendous evil, faces down a demon who wants to initiate the apocalypse.

events.title: Stepping Into the Unknown: Films From the Bob Dylan Center
events.description: Join us for a screening and conversation with Bob Dylan Center Director Steven Jenkins. Spanning decades and musical styles, this far-ranging one-hour program of short films and videos from the Bob Dylan Archive features rare and previously unreleased clips of Dylan on stage and in the studio.

events.title: Turner & Constable
events.description: Two of Britain’s greatest painters, J.M.W. Turner and John Constable were also the greatest of rivals. Celebrating the 250th anniversary of their births, with Turner born in 1775 and Constable in 1776, this film explores their lives and legacies alongside a groundbreaking Tate Britain exhibition.

events.title: 413 Day
events.description: A celebration of western Mass. with NEPM!

events.title: High School II
events.description: HIGH SCHOOL II is a film about Central Park East Secondary School (CPESS), a successful alternative high school in New York's Spanish Harlem, 85-95% of whose graduates go on to four year colleges. New 4K restoration.

events.title: NT Live: The Audience
events.description: Academy Award winner Helen Mirren plays Queen Elizabeth II in the Olivier Award-winning hit production that inspired Netflix’s The Crown, returning to cinemas for the first time in over a decade.

events.title: Lady Snowblood
events.description: Gory revenge is raised to the level of visual poetry in this endlessly inventive film, charting the path of vengeance taken by a young woman whose parents were the victims of a criminal gang.

events.title: An Evening with Fran Lebowitz
events.description: Presented by NEPM's Smart & Funny People.

events.title: High School II
events.description: HIGH SCHOOL II is a film about Central Park East Secondary School (CPESS), a successful alternative high school in New York's Spanish Harlem, 85-95% of whose graduates go on to four year colleges. New 4K restoration.

events.title: Days and Nights in the Forest
events.description: DAYS AND NIGHTS IN THE FOREST is one of director Satyajit Ray’s greatest achievements, a modern search for connection that conjures the timeless resonance of a folktale. New 4K restoration.

events.title: Days and Nights in the Forest
events.description: DAYS AND NIGHTS IN THE FOREST is one of director Satyajit Ray’s greatest achievements, a modern search for connection that conjures the timeless resonance of a folktale. New 4K restoration.

events.title: WTO/99
events.description: This immersive archival documentary depicts the four-day clash between the then-emerging World Trade Organization and the 40,000+ people who took to the streets of Seattle in 1999 to protest. Editor/producer Alex Megaro joins us for a post-film discussion. Free to Amherst Cinema Members.

events.title: Funeral Parade of Roses
events.description: A key work of the Japanese New Wave and of queer cinema, this kaleidoscopic masterpiece is a headlong dive into the Tokyo night-world of drag queen bars, fueled by drugs, fuzz guitars, and black mascara.

events.title: Paddington 2
events.description: Paddington, now happily settled with the Brown family and a popular member of the local community, picks up a series of odd jobs to buy the perfect present for his Aunt Lucy's 100th birthday, only for the gift to be stolen.

events.title: Satantango
events.description: A seminal work of “slow cinema” by the late Béla Tarr, SÁTÁNTANGÓ is based on the book by Nobel Prize winner László Krasznahorkai. The story follows members of a small, defunct agricultural collective living in a post-apocalyptic landscape after the fall of Communism. This screening includes two intermissions.

events.title: A Matter of Life and Death
events.description: A British wartime aviator who cheats death must argue for his life before a celestial court, hoping to prolong his fledgling romance with an American radio operator. This cosmic romance alternates between rich Technicolor scenes on earth and a starkly beautiful, modernist black-and-white afterlife. Post-film discussion with poet Peter Gizzi.

events.title: NEPM Book Club: 'The Emperor of Gladness' with author Ocean Vuong
events.description: The NEPM Book Club's next meeting will be Thursday, April 30 at 7 p.m. We'll be reading "The Emperor of Gladness" by Ocean Vuong, who will be joining us for a Q&A!

events.title: The Blair Witch Project
events.description: A bona fide cultural phenomenon upon release, this found footage flick features three student filmmakers making a movie about a fabled witch credited with a string of murders dating back 200 years.

events.title: NT Live: The Audience
events.description: Academy Award winner Helen Mirren plays Queen Elizabeth II in the Olivier Award-winning hit production that inspired Netflix’s The Crown, returning to cinemas for the first time in over a decade.

events.title: In Jackson Heights
events.description: Frederick Wiseman's IN JACKSON HEIGHTS is about the racially and ethnically diverse neighborhood of Jackson Heights in Queens, New York.

events.title: NT Live: All My Sons (2026)
events.description: Bryan Cranston and Marianne Jean-Baptiste feature in the triumphantly acclaimed new production of Arthur Miller’s classic play.

events.title: Tenebrae
events.description: Following supernatural films SUSPIRIA and INFERNO, Dario Argento returned to his giallo roots with this slasher about a horror novelist and a copycat killer, featuring a classic synth score by Goblin.

events.title: In Jackson Heights
events.description: Frederick Wiseman's IN JACKSON HEIGHTS is about the racially and ethnically diverse neighborhood of Jackson Heights in Queens, New York.

events.title: Nadja
events.description: Executive produced by David Lynch, this cult film centers on NYC-based vampire Nadja following her father’s death at the hands of Dr. Van Helsing. Fusing B&W 35mm with hallucinatory Pixelvision video, the story merges elements from André Breton’s surrealist novel Nadja and the 1936 film DRACULA’S DAUGHTER. New 4K restoration.

events.title: Nadja
events.description: Executive produced by David Lynch, this cult film centers on NYC-based vampire Nadja following her father’s death at the hands of Dr. Van Helsing. Fusing B&W 35mm with hallucinatory Pixelvision video, the story merges elements from André Breton’s surrealist novel Nadja and the 1936 film DRACULA’S DAUGHTER. New 4K restoration.

events.title: Multiple Maniacs
events.description: John Waters’ gloriously grotesque second feature is replete with all manner of depravity, from robbery to murder to one of cinema’s most memorably blasphemous moments.

events.title: Queen Kelly and “The Delicate Art of Film Restoration”
events.description: Milestone Films co-founders Dennis Doros and Amy Heller join us in person to discuss the art of film restoration and the journey of QUEEN KELLY, a decades-long passion project that resurrected one of history’s legendary unfinished films, followed by a screening of the the new restoration.

events.title: Frida Kahlo
events.description: Who was Frida Kahlo? Everyone knows her name, but who was the woman behind the bright colors, the big brows, and the floral crowns? This 2020 film returns with new footage from a 2026 transatlantic exhibition from Tate Britain and MFA Houston.

events.title: Wolfwalkers
events.description: A young apprentice hunter and her father journey to Ireland to help wipe out the last wolf pack. But everything changes when she befriends a free-spirited girl from a mysterious tribe rumored to transform into wolves by night.

events.title: The City Dark
events.description: After moving to NYC from rural Maine, filmmaker Ian Cheney asks a simple question: do we need the stars? Introduction by James Lowenthal, Professor of Astronomy, Smith College.

events.title: NT Live: The Playboy of the Western World
events.description: Nicola Coughlan (Bridgerton) joins Éanna Hardwicke (The Sixth Commandment) and Siobhán McSweeney (Derry Girls) in John Millington Synge’s riveting play of youth and self-discovery.

events.title: Ear Hustle Live!
events.description: NEPM's Smart & Funny People series brings the acclaimed podcast Ear Hustle to the Iron Horse on Saturday, May 30.

events.title: Frida Kahlo
events.description: Who was Frida Kahlo? Everyone knows her name, but who was the woman behind the bright colors, the big brows, and the floral crowns? This 2020 film returns with new footage from a 2026 transatlantic exhibition from Tate Britain and MFA Houston.
